channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Republicans blast 'joke' sentencing of Trump 10 days before swearing in
item.description: President-elect Trump was sentenced for falsifying records just 10 days before he is set to take office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge will weigh holding Giuliani in contempt of court after jury's $148 million defamation award
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge is set to hear arguments Friday on whether to hold Rudy Giuliani in contempt of court for continuing to spread lies about two Georgia election workers after they secured a $148 million defamation judgment against him. Plaintiffs’ attorneys have asked U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Donald Trump sentenced with no penalty in New York criminal trial, as judge wishes him 'Godspeed' in 2nd term
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump was sentenced Friday after being found guilty on charges of falsifying business records stemming from Manhattan District Attorney Alvin Bragg’s yearslong investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden administration extends temporary status for more than 200,000 from El Salvador for 18 months
item.description: The Homeland Security Department says more than 200,000 Salvadorans who have lived more than two decades in the United States can remain in the country another 18 months, marking one of the Biden administration’s final acts on immigration policy.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Investigation clears Duluth Mayor’s campaign manager and partner
item.description: An external investigation commissioned by the city of Duluth has found Mayor Roger Reinert’s partner and campaign manager Amber Gurske did not act improperly through her communications with city staff early in his administration.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: President-elect Trump appears virtually in court to be sentenced in hush money case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is appearing virtually in a New York courtroom to be sentenced for his hush money conviction, 10 days before he is inaugurated.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump set for sentencing in his New York felony conviction
item.description: Trump on Thursday exhausted his last legal maneuver to stop the sentencing, after a narrow majority of Supreme Court justices declined to intervene.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Trump to be sentenced in New York criminal trial
item.description: President-elect Trump is expected to be sentenced Friday after being found guilty on charges of falsifying business records stemming from Manhattan District Attorney Alvin Bragg’s years-long investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance resigns from Senate as he and Trump prepare to take office
item.description: Vice President-elect JD Vance resigns from the U.S. Senate as he and President-elect Donald Trump prepare to take office on Jan. 20, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden to deliver a capstone address on his foreign policy legacy as he makes way for Trump's return
item.description: The White House says President Joe Biden will deliver a capstone address to highlight some of his administration's most significant foreign policy achievements.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Red state AGs welcome Trump crackdown on illegal immigration after four years battling Biden
item.description: Twenty Republican state attorneys general back Trump's impending immigration crackdown, criticizing Biden's policies for weakening border security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump accuses Newsom of prioritizing endangered fish species over protecting residents from wildfires
item.description: President-elect Trump launched a debate over who is to blame for California's wildfires after he accused Gov. Gavin Newsom of caring more about protecting an endangered fish species than protecting his residents from wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michelle Obama 'sends her thoughts and prayers' while absent from Carter's funeral
item.description: Michelle Obama wasn't among the mourners attending former President Carter's funeral service in Washington, but she says she's thinking of and praying for "everyone who loved and learned" from the late commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: 7 notable moments between VIPs at Carter’s funeral
item.description: Former President Carter’s funeral service at Washington National Cathedral brought together all five living presidents and current and former officials for an event full of notable interactions.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: The Biden administration succeeds in temporarily blocking a plea deal for accused 9/11 mastermind
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration succeeded Thursday in temporarily blocking accused 9/11 mastermind Khalid Sheikh Mohammed from entering a guilty plea in a deal that would spare him the risk of execution for al-Qaida’s Sept. 11, 2001, attacks.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: A divided Supreme Court on Thursday rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s bid to delay his sentencing in his hush money case in New York.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, MICHAEL HILL and MICHAEL R. SISAK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats join Republicans in voting to advance bill to detain migrants accused of crimes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Newly in the minority, Democrats voted with Republicans on Thursday to advance legislation that would require federal authorities to detain unauthorized immigrants who have been accused of certain crimes — signaling that they will try and find spots to work with President-elect Don
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House passes ICC sanctions bill; GOP Senate leader promises to bring it to floor
item.description: The House on Thursday passed legislation to sanction officials with the International Criminal Court (ICC), pushing back against the court's decision to issue arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and his former defense minister for their actions during Israel’s war against H
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lack of federal funds may mean no EBT replacements for scam victims
item.description: Many parents have reported benefits being erased from EBT cards in the weeks since Congress declined to continue funding.
item.dc:creator: Sean C. Davis, NewsNation

item.title: Biden cancels trip to Italy, meant as final foreign visit of presidency, as fires rage in California
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden canceled the final overseas trip of his presidency just hours before he was set to depart for Rome and the Vatican, choosing to remain in Washington to monitor the response to devastating fires raging in California.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter remembered by colleagues, family and friends as a devoted public servant
item.description: President Biden delivered a eulogy to former President Jimmy Carter at the National Cathedral service, noting the 39th president's faith and dedication to service.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: NY governor proposes $110 million for new child care centers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Continuing a theme of proposing splashy and spendy new proposals aimed at keeping families in New York, Gov. Kathy Hochul took aim at the lack of child care centers Tuesday. Hochul wants to create a $110 million fund to support the construction of new child care facilities.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Curator talks archiving Walz’s bid for VP, preserving political legacies
item.description: While brief, Gov. Tim Walz’s bid for vice president will add another chapter to his legacy in politics, and the Minnesota Historical Society has gotten a jumpstart on preserving it.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Ex-President Jimmy Carter's body arrives in Washington
item.description: The next phase of former President Jimmy Carter's state funeral has begun as his body is transported to Washington.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: Appeals court rejects Trump’s latest attempt to get Friday’s hush money sentencing called off
item.description: In a one-sentence ruling following an emergency hearing, Judge Ellen Gesmer denied Trump’s request for an immediate order that would spare him from being sentenced while he appeals Judge Juan M. Merchan’s decision last week to uphold the historic verdict.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Hush money judge refuses to delay Trump's criminal sentencing
item.description: The New York judge who oversaw President-elect Trump’s criminal trial last spring declined his request to pause Friday’s sentencing, setting up a last-minute appellate battle as he seeks to stave off the proceeding.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Biden blocks some new offshore drilling ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: President Biden has blocked new offshore drilling for oil and gas in several parts of the country about two weeks before President-elect Trump takes office. Biden has announced that he’ll block new drilling off the entire East Coast, as well as California, Oregon and Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: 23-year-old hopeful files to run for Tampa mayor
item.description: The next mayoral race is not until 2027. For Alan Henderson, he hopes that’s enough time for people to know his name and what he hopes to accomplish, if elected.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: With Board Chair Nora Vargas ending her term, here's who's vying to replace her
item.description: As of Friday, there are four people who have shown interest in running to replace Chair Nora Vargas on the San Diego County Board of Supervisors at the end of her term.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5
